rag_rules: Repair type mismatches in AGREE expressions by ensuring all operands in comparisons and arithmetic are of compatible types (int, real, bool) and that helper variables are explicitly typed.
Fix unresolved references by eliminating guarantees or assumptions that reference non-visible AADL features, properties, or undeclared identifiers.
generic_rules: Use eq declarations to create typed intermediaries for complex expressions to avoid type propagation errors.
Guard stateful comparisons (e.g., pre()) with initialized flags to prevent invalid temporal logic.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Binary operators (>=, =, -, +) require left and right operands of the same type (int/int, real/real, bool/bool).
AGREE expressions must not directly reference AADL property names or unit tokens as variables.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to auto-convert AADL properties into AGREE variables; they are not in scope unless explicitly exposed via ports or constants.
Avoid nested if-then-else without proper parentheses or semicolon termination; mismatched keywords indicate structural syntax errors.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
